title: [trd:team-based-execution-model:task:TRD-025] Implement parallel builder execution
description:
File: packages/development/commands/implement-trd-beads.yaml (Execute phase)

Actions:
1. Lead maintains concurrent builder slot count (default: 2, configurable via max parallel N)
2. When multiple tasks available, lead assigns up to N tasks to different builders simultaneously
3. Each builder operates independently with isolated file changes
4. Use existing file conflict detection to avoid assigning tasks with overlapping file targets
5. Builders work in parallel via concurrent Task() delegations
6. Review and QA remain sequential per task

title: Build headless SDK for programmatic agent invocation
description: Inspired by GSD SDK. TypeScript SDK with CLI/WebSocket transport for programmatic agent spawning, command execution, and event streaming. Enables CI/CD integration, custom tooling, and automated workflows without a terminal. New package packages/sdk/.

title: [epic] Community plugin catalog and extension system
description: Build an open community contribution mechanism for ensemble plugins — submission guidelines, catalog listing, and CI validation. Currently the marketplace is entirely Fortium-curated with no third-party contribution path. Mirrors spec-kit's community catalog that includes Azure DevOps integration, archive extensions, etc. Grows the ecosystem beyond what Fortium can maintain alone.

title: [epic] [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] discipline in create-prd
description: Modify create-prd to mandate that the generating agent marks every ambiguity with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION: specific question] rather than making assumptions. Clarification markers become the structured agenda for the refine-prd interview — eliminating silent best-guess resolutions. Mirrors spec-kit's built-in template discipline. Gap: ensemble PRD generation does not currently impose this constraint.

Two new commands: /ensemble:discover-standards (analyze codebase, extract conventions into standards/ directory with index.yml) and /ensemble:inject-standards (selectively inject relevant standards into agent context based on current task). Index-based approach for token efficiency. Could be new package packages/standards/ or added to packages/core/.

title: [epic] Multi-AI-agent support (Gemini, Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf)

Currently ensemble works only with Claude Code; the OpenCode translation layer is supplemental. Mirrors spec-kit's 20+ agent support model. Would dramatically expand addressable teams in organizations with heterogeneous tooling.
